title: Blend a CatBoost specialist with raw high-cardinality categories
summary: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered target statistics are designed to learn from high-cardinality categorical identities without the prediction shift of naive target encoding.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered target statistics are designed to learn from high-cardinality categorical identities without the prediction shift of naive target encoding. This directly fits Driver/Race/Compound/Stint interactions that the current manual frequency features only approximate.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost classifier on mostly raw categorical columns plus a lean set of causal numeric features; include raw combos like Driver×Race, Driver×Compound, Race×Compound, Year×Race. Validate only with race-group folds and blend OOF probabilities with the existing tree model.
expected_effect: May recover signal from sparse identity interactions and race-specific strategy habits that manual encodings miss, improving ensemble AUC more than replacing the current model outright.
risk: If folds are not strictly grouped by race/weekend, ordered categorical gains can be overstated. CatBoost may also add little if current engineered numerics already explain most variance.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
